name: Private Capital Markets Report
covers: Private-company valuation multiples and cost of capital; recast-EBITDA as the dominant method; deal-killer reasons (valuation gap, unreasonable demands, no market)

name: Market Pulse Survey
covers: Lower-middle-market ($2M-$50M) sale multiples, time on market, sale-to-asking ratio, financial vs strategic buyer mix, seller sentiment

name: DealStats Value Index (free quarterly summary)
covers: Realized private-company transaction multiple trends by size

name: Middle Market M&A Valuations Index
covers: Middle-market EBITDA purchase multiples and the size-premium gradient

name: State of Owner Readiness / Value Acceleration Methodology
covers: Owner-readiness statistics; the three-legs-of-the-stool (business/personal/financial) framework and Discover-Prepare-Decide gates

name: The Value Builder System (eight value drivers)
covers: Cross-industry value drivers: recurring revenue, customer concentration (Switzerland Structure), cash-flow, hierarchy of recurring revenue

name: Quality-of-Earnings practice guidance
covers: QoE methodology; addback substantiation; unsupported addbacks as the leading source of post-LOI price reductions

name: Financial due diligence / QoE guidance
covers: Financial due diligence scope: normalized income statement, revenue testing, working-capital review, debt-like items

name: Practitioner consensus (reputable M&A advisory insight libraries)
covers: Customer-concentration thresholds (10/20/30/40%), working-capital peg mechanics, deal-structure norms — used to corroborate, never as the sole authority for a seeded number
